entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in the Sahelian region.

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area.

entities.name: MILLET GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The specific yield of grains from pearl millet.

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of grain yield to total above-ground biomass.

entities.name: DRY MASS LOSS
entities.description: The reduction in mass of organic material during decomposition.

entities.name: ACACIA TUMIDA
entities.description: A tree species whose prunings are used as organic mulch.

entities.name: TERMITE
entities.description: Insects that contribute to the decomposition of organic materials.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the field study was conducted.

entities.name: SAHEL
entities.description: The semi-arid region of Africa where the study is applicable.

entities.name: SAHELIAN WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The broader geographic region of the study.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENT
entities.description: The overall experimental approach conducted in the field.

entities.name: LITTERBAG METHODOLOGY
entities.description: A technique used to measure the decomposition of organic matter.

entities.name: SINGLE EXPONENTIAL DECAY EQUATIONS
entities.description: Mathematical models used to analyze decomposition data.

entities.name: DECOMPOSITION
entities.description: The process of organic materials breaking down into simpler substances.

entities.name: MULCHING EFFECT
entities.description: The impact of applying organic materials on the soil surface to improve yield.

entities.name: NUTRIENT RELEASE
entities.description: The process by which nutrients are released from decomposing organic matter.

entities.name: MINERALIZATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting organic nutrients into inorganic forms.

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE DYNAMICS
entities.description: The changes in soil water content over time.

entities.name: FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: The natural environmental settings of the study.

entities.name: LOW-INPUT CROPPING SYSTEM
entities.description: A farming system characterized by minimal use of external inputs.

entities.name: NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Essential elements including Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), and Potassium (K).

entities.name: MILLET STRAW
entities.description: Straw from millet used as organic mulch.

relationships.description: Mulching with Acacia tumida prunings increases pearl millet grain yield
Mulching with millet straw increases pearl millet grain yield
Termites contribute significantly to the decomposition of organic materials
Soil moisture dynamics are positively correlated with mass losses during decomposition
Litterbag methodology is used to measure dry mass losses during decomposition
Field experiments were used to assess the yield of pearl millet
Single exponential decay equations were used to model decomposition data
Mineralization is the process that enables nutrient release from organic materials
Nutrient release from organic materials enhances crop yield
Acacia tumida prunings release N, P, and K during decomposition
Millet straw releases N, P, and K during decomposition
The field experiment was conducted in Niger
Low-input cropping systems are characteristic of the Sahel region
The mulching effect of organic materials increases millet grain yield
